result:
Done and verified end-to-end. The app now has a full RBAC system replacing the old hardcoded `owner`/`member` column.

**Data model** (`src/db.ts`): a `roles` table (per-workspace, with a `permissions text[]`, `is_system` flag, and case-insensitive unique names) and a `member_roles` join table so members can hold multiple roles. A composite foreign key `(role_id, workspace_id)` makes it impossible to assign a role from a different workspace. The migration is idempotent: it seeds ADMIN/GUEST system roles for every workspace, maps legacy `owner`‚ÜíADMIN and `member`‚ÜíGUEST, and drops the old `role` column ‚Äî I verified this ran correctly against the existing dev database.

**Permissions** (`src/permissions.ts`): a catalog of `members:read`, `members:invite`, `roles:read`, `roles:manage`. ADMIN holds the `*` wildcard (so it automatically covers future permissions) and GUEST holds the two read permissions. System role permissions cannot be edited, and system role names are reserved.

**Enforcement** (`src/routes/workspaces.ts`): every `/workspaces/:id/...` route now goes through `requirePermission()`, which returns 404 for non-members (preserving the existing anti-probing behavior) and 403 for members lacking the permission. A member's effective permissions are the union across all their roles. Creating a workspace grants the creator ADMIN; accepting an invitation grants GUEST; inviting now requires `members:invite` instead of "owner".

**New endpoints**, all gated by `roles:manage` (plus `GET /workspaces/:id/roles` for listing, gated by `roles:read`):
- `POST /workspaces/:id/roles` ‚Äî create a custom role (409 on duplicate name)
- `PUT /workspaces/:id/roles/:roleId/permissions` ‚Äî set a custom role's permissions
- `POST /workspaces/:id/members/:userId/roles` ‚Äî assign a role (idempotent)
- `DELETE /workspaces/:id/members/:userId/roles/:roleId` ‚Äî remove a role, with a transaction-serialized last-admin guard that refuses (409) to strip the final ADMIN from a workspace

Typecheck passes, and I smoke-tested the full flow live: invite/accept, guest denied then granted invite rights via a custom "Recruiter" role, multi-role membership, duplicate/reserved name rejection, system-role edit rejection, last-admin protection, and outsider 404s. The changes are uncommitted on `main` ‚Äî say the word if you want them committed.
